behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure (missing a taxonomy label from the API request payload) can be caught by mocking the external API client and inspecting the arguments it was called with. No I/O or real API needed — the payload construction is a local deterministic operation. Verify that `all(label in api_call_args['labels'] for label in TAXONOMY)` holds.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client, call classify(document), and assert that the mock was called with a payload containing all labels from the taxonomy. Verify with `assert_called_with` or by inspecting `call_args`.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure (returning an out-of-taxonomy label) is logic local to the classification module. Mock the external API to return an unexpected label, then verify the module returns 'unknown'. This is deterministic validation logic that does not depend on real API behavior or external state.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a label like 'malware' (not in the fixed taxonomy). Call classify(document, mocked_api) and assert the result equals 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on whether the module implements caching or relies on the external API to return deterministic results. If the module caches (testable at Unit with a mocked API by verifying the API is called only once), that's sufficient. However, if correctness relies on the external AI API returning the same label for the same document on repeated calls, the real failure only manifests with a real API whose behavior is non-deterministic. The safe minimum is Integration: test with the real external API (or a real API stub that reflects actual non-determinism) to verify either caching works or the API is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify(same_document) twice and assert both results are identical. If testing with a mocked API, verify the API is only called once (proving caching). If testing with a real external API, run the test multiple times to detect non-determinism in the API response.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that actual token consumption exceeds the budget when measured by the real external API's tokenizer. A pure Unit test with mocked token counts (e.g., mock_api.returns(token_count=100)) will pass but real-world usage fails because the actual prompt + document + taxonomy encoding consumes more tokens. To catch this real failure, the test must either use the real external API to measure actual token consumption, or use a real tokenizer that matches the API's tokenization (Integration level). Mocking hides the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify(typical_document, budget=1000) and either: (a) make a real API call and verify the returned token_count is <= 1000, or (b) use a real tokenizer matching the API's algorithm (e.g., OpenAI's tokenizer) to measure the prompt token count and verify it stays within budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The retry logic itself (detection of rate-limit status, waiting, and retrying) is testable with a mocked API that returns a rate-limit error on the first call and succeeds on the second. The wait behavior can be verified by mocking `time.sleep` or using a time-mocking library (e.g., freezegun) to test without actual delays. The failure (module returns error immediately instead of retrying) is caught by the mock sequence. Real wait times and backoff algorithms can be validated at System level, but the minimum to catch the core failure is Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to raise a rate-limit exception (or return HTTP 429) on the first call and succeed on the second call. Mock time.sleep to avoid real waiting. Call classify(document) and verify: (1) the method does not raise an exception, (2) the result is valid, and (3) the mocked API was called at least twice. Optionally verify the wait duration by checking sleep() was called with appropriate backoff.
